title: Evil is absolute, good is relative in Romanian politics
summary: The article argues that Romanian democracy is endangered because corrupt political forces unite effectively (absolute evil) while well-intentioned citizens remain divided by their own critical standards (relative good). The author identifies lack of education and critical thinking as the root cause of vulnerability to propaganda, referencing the 2024 presidential election social media manipulation, and proposes USR as a potential reformist solution before the 2028 elections.
talking_points: Romanian democracy is threatened by united corrupt political forces that exploit poor education and emotional manipulation of neglected populations
The 2024 presidential elections exposed Romania's vulnerability to social media propaganda, and the effects persist even outside electoral periods
Critical thinking, while essential against manipulation, paradoxically divides well-intentioned citizens who hold each other to impossibly high standards
USR could lead a reformist government by 2028 if it reunites former members and returns to meritocratic principles, potentially exceeding 22% as in 2019 EU elections
Without reform, Romania faces a dark period under AUR and PSD with retrograde policies, similar to Trump's rapid transformation of the US
author: Florin Burta
